text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. The goal is to make the code cleaner, easier to read, and simpler to maintain. For example, you might rename variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicated logic. Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs—it focuses on the internal quality of the code. Over time, without regular refactoring, code can become messy and hard to work with. This is known as technical debt, which slows down development and increases the risk of errors. By refactoring often, developers reduce this debt and keep the codebase healthy and efficient.
